q: Covalent bonding can occur between similar or even ___ atoms.
The concept of ___ rather than electron transfer explains bonding between similar or identical atoms.
A covalent bond is the electrostatic attraction between the ___ and the shared electrons.
Covalent bonding usually occurs between ___.
In covalent bonding, the outermost orbitals of the atoms overlap so that ___ in each of the bonding atoms can be shared.
___ in each of the bonding atoms are not involved in bonding.
In single covalent bonds, only ___ of electrons is shared between two atoms.
A double bond is formed by sharing ___ of electrons between two atoms.
A triple bond is formed by sharing ___ of electrons.
The higher the number of bonded electrons, the stronger the attraction between the two atoms and the ___ the bond length.
A ___, or dative bond, is a special type of covalent bond.
A coordinate bond is characterised by the fact that the two shared electrons are both ___ of the participating atoms.
A coordinate bond is expressed as an ___ which indicates the direction in which the electrons are donated.
Molecules with incomplete octets are called ___.
Lewis structures are only ___ representations of how the valence shell electrons are arranged around individual atoms in a molecule or an ion.
Molecules that deviate from the octet rule, such as electron-deficient molecules and ___, can occur.
An expanded valence shell molecule has valence electrons of more than ___.
The distribution of electronic structure of a molecule can be explained using the ___ (MOT).
VSEPR stands for ___ theory.
This model assumes that the electron pairs will arrange themselves to ___ from one another.
These pairs of electrons may be expressed as ___ and/or bonding pairs that can influence the molecular shape.
The lone pairs are localised on the central atom while each ___ is shared between two atoms.
The repulsion between lone pairs of electrons is ___ as compared to the lone pair-bonding pair and bonding pair-bonding pair repulsions.
Repulsion effects result in deviations from the idealised shapes and alternations in ___ in molecules.
All ___ molecules represent two points in space and are joined by a straight line.
A triatomic molecule ($AX_2$ type) has a ___.
Some elements occur in solids where the atoms are held together by single covalent bonds, existing as ___.
In a diamond, each carbon atom is joined to ___ other carbon atoms by strong covalent bonds.
In diamond, the carbon atoms form a regular ___ network resulting in a giant structure.
In the case of graphite, each carbon atom is joined to the other ___ carbon atoms by covalent bonds.
Each layer in graphite is formed with ___ of carbon atoms.
There are weak ___ forces between the layers of carbon atoms in graphite.
Graphite can conduct electricity because it has ___ in each layer.
Fullerene ($C_{60}$), carbon nanotubes, and graphene do not exist as giant structures but are only ___.
Silicon(IV) oxide, $SiO_2$ (silica or quartz) is an example of a ___ molecule.
In quartz, each oxygen atom is bonded to only ___ silicon atoms.
Quartz does not conduct electricity because it has no ___.
Bond angle is an angle between any ___ that include a common atom, usually measured in degrees.
